condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I approached this as a short-window, status-quo question: would Rajasthan's State Election Commission (SEC) announce a fixed panchayat (PRI) general election polling schedule within roughly an 11-day window (before 23:59 IST July 24, 2026)? I ran web searches targeting the SEC schedule, the OBC reservation process, and the court-mandated timeline.

## Key Evidence

My searches surfaced a decisive causal chain:

- **OBC reservation survey timeline**: Per `newkerala`, the Rajasthan OBC Commission began a statewide door-to-door survey on **July 10**, running until **July 23**, "after which the Commission will compile its findings and submit a report to the state government." The reservation report thus lands only *after* July 23 — the very end of the resolution window.
- **90-day lead requirement**: Per `panchayattimes` (Hindi), the SEC formally told the Panchayati Raj department it needs **at least 90 days** to conduct elections, and this clock starts only once SC/ST/OBC/women reservation is finalized.
- **Status quo / delays**: No schedule had been announced; reporting framed the situation as "won't happen by July 31" and pointed to OBC reservation as the cause of delay, with polls potentially slipping to **September–October**.
- **Judicial pressure**: The Rajasthan High Court (May 22 order) set a **July 31 completion deadline**, and context (Supreme Court, April 2026 assurances) confirmed an ongoing contested delimitation/reservation dispute. This creates a genuine but small upside pathway — the SEC could invoke a fallback (treating OBC-reserved seats as general) and issue a schedule abruptly under pressure.

## Conclusion

I built four factors: three negative barriers (survey-report timing, 90-day lead, status-quo delays) and one small positive (HC deadline + fallback pathway, logit +0.6, confidence 0.5). With the prerequisite reservation process not even concluding until July 23 and the report submitted afterward, announcing an actual polling date before July 24 is highly improbable. I ran a premortem which returned a **warn** verdict — the core evidence chain verified against the trace, with an independent estimate of ~4–8% consistent with mine. I concluded **5% (NO)**, logit -3, keeping the estimate but slightly widening confidence per the reviewer's note.

capability_gaps: No direct fetch of the SEC official notifications page (sec.rajasthan.gov.in/AllNotificationsNew.aspx) appeared in the captured trace to confirm no schedule was live at forecast time — would have hardened the status-quo factor
subtle_bugs: The trace rendering captured only 2 web_search calls while the agent's factors reference details (OBC-as-general fallback, Sept-Oct slip) not visible in the rendered searches, implying uncaptured search results the reviewer could not fully verify

classification.approach: Qualitative status-quo analysis of a court-mandated election-scheduling process, keyed on the OBC reservation survey timeline that gates any SEC schedule announcement within the resolution window.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether the High Court's July 31 deadline forces the SEC to invoke a fallback (treating OBC-reserved seats as general) and issue a schedule before the reservation report is finalized.

risk_flags.evidence: The official SEC notifications page (sec.rajasthan.gov.in/AllNotificationsNew.aspx) appeared in search results but was not fetched/read in the captured trace to directly confirm no PRI schedule was live at forecast time; the status-quo factor rests on secondary reporting.

confidence_in_estimate was 0.85 despite a live HC July-31 hard deadline and an unverified fallback pathway (treat OBC seats as general) that the agent itself cites as a coherent YES route; Indian SECs can notify on short notice. The premortem reviewer flagged 0.75-0.80 as better calibrated.

strengths: Identified the single decisive gating fact quickly: the OBC survey runs July 10-23 with the report submitted only AFTER July 23, meaning the prerequisite for any schedule cannot conclude until the very end of the resolution window.

Correctly distinguished a preparatory/reservation step from the resolution requirement of a FIXED polling date within the window, respecting the question's fine print rather than treating any election-related activity as a YES.
Ran a substantive premortem that built a genuine YES pathway (HC July-31 deadline + fallback + short-notice SEC behavior) and verified each factor against the trace, rather than a token restatement.
Kept a sensible residual 5% for the HC-pressure surprise scenario instead of collapsing to a near-zero estimate that would ignore the live court deadline.
weaknesses: Did not fetch the official SEC notifications page that appeared in results to directly confirm no schedule was live at forecast time, leaving the status-quo factor on secondary sourcing.

Cited two details (the OBC-as-general fallback mechanism and the Sept-Oct slip) that are not visible in the rendered trace, making them unverifiable from the captured evidence even though they were used in factor descriptions and the summary.
Self-reported the run as clean and 'Sufficient for a confident NO' without disclosing the session error activity the premortem reviewer surfaced; the tool_audit narrative overstated a clean run.
Relied heavily on Hindi-language snippets read at the snippet level rather than fetching full articles to confirm the exact 90-day-lead and fallback wording.

issues: Trace rendering captured only 2 of the web_search calls, so details referenced in the factors (fallback mechanism, Sept-Oct slip) could not be verified from the visible trace — a trace-capture completeness gap.
The agent's tool_audit narrative claimed a clean run while the premortem reviewer noted session error activity, indicating the self-audit did not reflect ground-truth metrics.
A Bash command in the nested premortem agent was denied by a PreToolUse hook, blocking a worldview directory listing (minor, did not affect the review).
